title: Clone GFP into pUC19
description: The donor plasmid carries a GFP gene flanked by EcoRI and BamHI sites. Cut both plasmids with the same two enzymes, then drag the GFP fragment into the vector backbone.
teaching_point: Using two different enzymes (EcoRI + BamHI) creates incompatible ends on each side — the insert can only ligate in one orientation.

donor.name: pDonor-GFP
donor.sequence_note: synthetic 3200 bp donor with GFP flanked by EcoRI and BamHI in the MCS

title: Directional cloning with KpnI / HindIII
description: Clone a luciferase reporter into pUC19 using KpnI and HindIII. These enzymes create different overhangs, guaranteeing a single orientation.
teaching_point: KpnI leaves a 3' overhang (GTAC) while HindIII leaves a 5' overhang (AGCT). Incompatible end types prevent reverse-orientation ligation.

title: Single-enzyme cloning (non-directional)
description: Clone a resistance cassette using only EcoRI. Because both ends are identical, the insert can ligate in either orientation — predict how many ligation products are possible.
teaching_point: Single-enzyme cloning with a palindromic site creates two possible insert orientations and one self-ligation product. Colony screening by PCR or restriction digest is required.

title: Three-fragment digest
description: The donor plasmid has three EcoRI sites. Select the fragment containing mCherry and discard the rest.
teaching_point: Partial digests and multi-fragment gels require careful identification of the fragment of interest by size and position.
